uncertain_site_assignment.locality_text: Ancient European dog CTC sample context
uncertain_site_assignment.location_evidence_text: The older specimen, which we refer to hereafter as HXH, was found at the Early Neolithic site of Herxheim and is dated to 5,223-5,040 cal. BCE. The younger specimen, CTC, was found in Cherry Tree Cave and corresponds to the End Neolithic period in Central Europe.
uncertain_site_assignment.site_name: Ancient European dog CTC sample context
uncertain_site_assignment.review_note: Current evidence stays at region or transect scale and must not masquerade as exact sample-site truth.
No location evidence has been extracted yet for this recovered sample row.

region_only_geography.confidence_rationale: The current local evidence names a multi-cave Galician context rather than one site, so the row stays refused from point mapping.
region_only_geography.support_gap_note: Primary paper linkage is still missing locally, and the archive description only supports regional Galicia context.

region_only_geography.site_label: Ancient European dog CTC sample context
region_only_geography.original_place_text: Herxheim and Cherry Tree Cave ancient dog contexts
region_only_geography.confidence_rationale: The archived source names multiple dog sites, but the current shipped row still aggregates them, so central-Europe coordinates are refused rather than published as if they were one site.
region_only_geography.support_gap_note: Cherry Tree Cave and Herxheim are named, but the current accession-backed row still collapses them into one aggregate context.

region_only_geography.site_label: Lake Qinghai Basin ancient goat context
region_only_geography.confidence_rationale: The current local source names the Lake Qinghai Basin rather than one excavation site, so point mapping would overstate precision.
region_only_geography.support_gap_note: The local paper capture supports region-level place naming rather than an excavation-grade site point.

region_only_geography.site_label: North African donkey domestication and spread transect
region_only_geography.original_place_text: Africa to Europe and Asia donkey spread context
region_only_geography.resolved_place_text: North Africa and Levant dispersal extent
region_only_geography.confidence_rationale: The local archive description supports Africa-to-Eurasia spread, not one donkey site with defensible point geometry.
region_only_geography.support_gap_note: No local paper is pinned yet for this project, so the current coordinate posture cannot go beyond refused regional context.

region_only_geography.site_label: North Africa to Europe domestic cat dispersal transect
region_only_geography.original_place_text: North Africa to Europe domestic cat dispersal transect
region_only_geography.resolved_place_text: North Africa to Europe dispersal extent
region_only_geography.confidence_rationale: The source supports a North Africa to Europe dispersal process, not one cat excavation point.
region_only_geography.support_gap_note: The current cat row is explicitly transregional and therefore refused from point publication.

region_only_geography.site_label: Baltic Sea Region short-tailed sheep context
region_only_geography.original_place_text: Baltic Sea Region short-tailed sheep context
region_only_geography.confidence_rationale: The local source archive currently supports Baltic-region posture, not one named sheep excavation site with defensible coordinates.
region_only_geography.support_gap_note: A readable local article or parsed supplement table is still missing for exact site extraction.

region_only_geography.confidence_rationale: The local archive supports Svalbard archipelago context rather than one named reindeer site, so the row stays refused from point mapping.

region_only_geography.site_label: Near East and Europe pig domestication transect
region_only_geography.original_place_text: Near East and Europe pig domestication transect
region_only_geography.resolved_place_text: Near East to Europe regional study extent
region_only_geography.confidence_rationale: The archived source supports a domestication transect across the Near East and Europe rather than one site, so point-level mapping would bluff precision.
region_only_geography.support_gap_note: The current pig lead is explicitly regional and therefore refused from point publication.
